name: Potential SSH Session via Malicious PAM
description: Detects signs of Plague malware activity targeting Linux systems by modifying Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM). The malware injects a rogue PAM module to intercept SSH credentials, manipulate session variables, and erase forensic traces. It survives system updates and operates stealthily by avoiding detection through standard logging mechanisms.

rules.name: Potential SSH Session via Malicious PAM

author: Mary Joy Ramos & Lance Albert Mendoza
